metadata.lyricist: William Williams, 1745.  Translated by Peter Williams, 1771
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2007 Revision.
c_lines: Words: William Williams, 1745.  Translated by Peter Williams, 1771.
Music: 'Cwm Rhondda' John Hughes, 1907.  Setting: "The Methodist Hymnbook" (UK), 1933.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2007 Revision.

1.raw_text: Guide me, O Thou great Jehovah, Pilgrim through this barren land. I am weak, but Thou art mighty; Hold me with Thy pow'rful hand. Bread of Heaven, Bread of Heaven, Feed me till I want no more; Feed me till I want no more.

2.raw_text: Open now the crystal fountain, Whence the healing stream doth flow; Let the fire and cloudy pillar Lead me all my journey through. Strong Deliv'rer, strong Deliv'rer, Be Thou still my Strength and Shield; Be Thou still my Strength and Shield.

3.raw_text: Lord, I trust Thy mighty power, Wondrous are Thy works of old; Thou deliver'st Thine from thralldom, Who for naught themselves had sold: Thou didst conquer, Thou didst conquer, Sin, and Satan and the grave, Sin, and Satan and the grave.

4.raw_text: When I tread the verge of Jordan, Bid my anxious fears subside; Death of deaths, and hell's destruction, Land me safe on Canaan's side. Songs of praises, songs of praises, I will ever give to Thee; I will ever give to Thee.

5.raw_text: Musing on my habitation, Musing on my heav'nly home, Fills my soul with holy longings: Come, my Jesus, quickly come; Vanity is all I see; Lord, I long to be with Thee! Lord, I long to be with Thee!
